tasks.description: Phase 1: Apply stopPropagation portal workaround to TimelineCanvas shot group menu. Read the existing workarounds in ClipAction.tsx:92-101 and SelectionContextMenu.tsx:84-91 for the pattern. Add the same useEffect + stopPropagation pattern to the shotGroupMenuRef in TimelineCanvas.tsx near line 288. Add a matching comment explaining the portal Node.contains() limitation. Verify all three menus now have consistent workaround code and comments.
tasks.executor_notes: Verified the TimelineCanvas shot-group menu now uses the same portal mousedown stopPropagation workaround/comment pattern as ClipAction, and SelectionContextMenu now carries the same portal-limitation comment. Covered by the shared portal-menu test run.

tasks.description: Phase 2: Make SQL authoritative for travel-between-images duplicate frame placement. (1) In useDuplicateAction.ts (~line 109), stop passing target_timeline_frame when duplicating from the timeline. Instead pass timeline_frame (current frame) and next_timeline_frame so the SQL's midpoint/+30 logic runs. (2) In useTimelineOrchestratorActions.ts:160-169, remove the calculateDuplicateFrame call and setPendingDuplicateFrame(finalFrame). Pass timelineFrame and the next image's frame to onImageDuplicate. (3) Update the onImageDuplicate callback signature through the chain (useShotSettingsValue.ts:246-248 → TimelineContainer.tsx → Timeline/index.tsx) to accept (imageId, currentFrame, nextFrame?) instead of (imageId, targetFrame). (4) Use the RPC's returned timeline_frame in the mutation's onSuccess to update UI position. (5) Add a doc comment to calculateDuplicateFrame in timelinePositionCalculator.ts stating: 'For optimistic/preview use. For duplicate_as_new_generation, SQL is authoritative — pass timeline_frame + next_timeline_frame instead of pre-computing target_timeline_frame.' (6) Update tests in useDuplicateAsNewGeneration.test.ts and any travel-between-images tests that assert on target_timeline_frame parameter passing.
tasks.executor_notes: Verified the travel-between-images duplicate path now forwards currentFrame + nextFrame and stops precomputing/passing target_timeline_frame. The optimistic duplicate marker now uses the source frame while the RPC-returned timeline_frame remains authoritative for the new item position. Duplicate RPC/unit tests passed.

tasks.description: Phase 3a: Extract stable assetKey→generationId mapping from registry in TimelineEditor.tsx. (1) Add a useMemo that extracts Record<string, string> from data.registry.assets, mapping each asset key to its generationId. (2) Create a useStableValue hook (inline, ~10 lines, shallow object comparison) to keep the same reference when keys/values haven't changed. (3) Update resolveSelectedGenerationIdsForShotCreation signature to accept assetGenerationMap: Record<string, string> instead of registry: AssetRegistry. Change line 54 from registry.assets[assetKey] to assetGenerationMap[assetKey]. (4) Update the selectionShotCreationState memo (line 234) to depend on [data?.rows, data?.meta, stableAssetGenerationMap, selectedClipIds] instead of [data, selectedClipIds]. (5) Verify handleCreateShotFromSelection and handleGenerateVideoFromSelection callbacks (lines 263-294) also stabilize.
tasks.executor_notes: Verified TimelineEditor now extracts a stable assetKey->generationId map, resolveSelectedGenerationIdsForShotCreation reads that plain Record, and selectionShotCreationState depends on data.rows/data.meta/stableAssetGenerationMap instead of the whole data object. TimelineEditor unit tests passed.

tasks.description: Phase 3b: Remove shotActionsSnapshotRef workaround from ClipAction.tsx. (1) Remove shotActionsSnapshotRef, latestShotActionsRef, the ShotActionSnapshot type (lines 50-58), and the snapshot capture logic (line 301). (2) Simplify menu rendering to use props directly instead of snapshot fallbacks (lines 412-417). (3) Remove snapshot-related parts of areClipActionPropsEqual if any. This depends on T3 completing so that the underlying re-render issue is fixed first.
tasks.executor_notes: Verified ClipAction now renders shot actions directly from live props with the snapshot refs/type/capture logic removed. Confirmed there are no remaining snapshot references via ripgrep, and the full ClipAction test module passed.

tasks.description: Phase 4: Eliminate post-upload fetch in createShotWithFilesPath. (1) Update processSingleDroppedImage return type (externalImageDrop.ts:283-350) to include full generation metadata: generationId, location, thumbnail_url, type, created_at, params, primary_variant_id, shot_generation_id, timeline_frame. The createGenerationForUploadedImage call at line 94 already does .select().single() which returns the full row — capture and return it. (2) Update processDroppedImages (externalImageDrop.ts:352-396) to accumulate per-file metadata into an array and include it in return value. (3) Update uploadToShot mutation (useShotCreation.ts:65-91) to surface the accumulated metadata in its return value. (4) Update createShotWithFilesPath (shotCreationPaths.ts:187-250): use generation metadata from upload result to build GenerationRow[] directly, remove the post-upload shot_generations fetch (lines 199-226), keep cache hydration but feed from upload result. (5) Check if mapJoinedShotGenerationToRow (shotCreationPaths.ts:78-112) is still used elsewhere — clean up if only used by removed fetch. (6) Update tests in shotCreationPaths.test.ts and externalImageDrop tests.
tasks.executor_notes: Verified the upload pipeline now returns generationMetadata per file, and createShotWithFilesPath hydrates cache directly from that metadata with no post-upload shot_generations fetch. Upload-path and shot-creation-path tests passed.

tasks.description: Run all affected tests and verify changes. Run vitest for each phase's affected test files: (1) ClipAction.test.tsx, SelectionContextMenu.test.tsx for Phase 1/3. (2) useDuplicateAsNewGeneration.test.ts for Phase 2. (3) shotCreationPaths.test.ts, useShotCreation.test.ts, cacheUtils.test.ts for Phase 4. If any test fails, read the error, fix the code, and re-run until all pass. Also write a short throwaway verification script that: (a) confirms resolveSelectedGenerationIdsForShotCreation accepts a plain Record<string,string> map and not the full registry, (b) confirms no target_timeline_frame is passed in the duplicate path, (c) confirms createShotWithFilesPath has no post-upload fetch call. Run it, verify, then delete.

watch_items: DEBT: video-editor-effect-registry — Phase 3 touches registry consumption patterns. Ensure the useStableValue extraction does not mask legitimate generationId changes when effects register new assets. The stableAssetGenerationMap must update when actual generationId assignments change, not just when _version bumps.

Assumption: processSingleDroppedImage's createGenerationForUploadedImage already does .select().single() returning the full row. Verify this before building Phase 4 — if it only returns the ID, the approach needs adjustment.
Assumption: addImageToShot / addImageToShotWithoutPosition RPCs return the created shot_generation row including timeline_frame. Verify early in T5.
Assumption: useStableValue (shallow object comparison) is either already in the codebase or trivial to add inline. Search for existing implementations before creating a new one.

The onImageDuplicate callback signature change in T2 threads through 4+ files (useShotSettingsValue → TimelineContainer → Timeline/index → useDuplicateAction). Trace the full chain before editing to avoid missing a callsite.
Phase 3 ordering matters: T3 (extract stable map) must complete before T4 (remove snapshot workaround), otherwise ClipAction menus will flicker during EffectRegistry re-renders.

Portal stopPropagation workaround (T1): the three menus (ClipAction, SelectionContextMenu, TimelineCanvas) should be the only portaled menus with custom click-outside handlers. Other menus use Radix/shadcn which handles portal containment internally. If you find additional custom portal menus, apply the workaround there too.

calculateDuplicateFrame and ensureUniqueFrame must remain available for non-RPC callers (addImageToShotHelpers, timelineDropHelpers, useTimelineCore, ShotImageManager/image-utils). Do not remove them — only add a doc comment.

sense_checks.question: Does the TimelineCanvas shot group menu now have the same stopPropagation useEffect pattern and portal-limitation comment as ClipAction.tsx and SelectionContextMenu.tsx?
sense_checks.executor_note: Verified TimelineCanvas, ClipAction, and SelectionContextMenu now share the same portaled-menu mousedown stopPropagation workaround and portal-limitation comment.

sense_checks.question: Is target_timeline_frame no longer passed from the travel-between-images duplicate path to the RPC? Are timeline_frame and next_timeline_frame passed instead, letting SQL compute placement?
sense_checks.executor_note: Verified the travel-between-images duplicate path no longer precomputes/passes target_timeline_frame. It now forwards timelineFrame + nextTimelineFrame to the RPC path and lets SQL resolve placement.

sense_checks.question: Does the selectionShotCreationState memo depend on stableAssetGenerationMap (a Record<string,string>) rather than the full data object, so registry _version bumps that don't change generationId mappings don't trigger recomputation?
sense_checks.executor_note: Verified selectionShotCreationState now depends on stableAssetGenerationMap instead of the full data object, so registry churn without generation-id mapping changes does not force recomputation.

sense_checks.question: Are shotActionsSnapshotRef, latestShotActionsRef, ShotActionSnapshot type, and all snapshot capture/fallback logic fully removed from ClipAction.tsx with no remaining references?
sense_checks.executor_note: Verified shotActionsSnapshotRef, latestShotActionsRef, ShotActionSnapshot, and the menu snapshot fallback logic are fully removed from ClipAction.tsx with no remaining references.

sense_checks.question: Does createShotWithFilesPath now use generation metadata returned from the upload pipeline for cache hydration, with zero post-upload queries to shot_generations?
sense_checks.executor_note: Verified createShotWithFilesPath hydrates from upload-returned generationMetadata and contains no post-upload shot_generations fetch.

sense_checks.question: Do all existing tests pass (vitest run for affected files), and has the throwaway verification script confirmed the three structural invariants before being deleted?

meta_commentary:
Execution guidance:

**Parallelism:** T1, T2, T3, and T5 are independent and can be worked in parallel. T4 must wait for T3. T6 must wait for all others.

**Biggest risk areas:**
- T2 (duplicate frame authority) has the widest blast radius — the callback signature change threads through 4+ files. Trace the full onImageDuplicate chain before editing. Pay attention to whether any callers outside the travel-between-images path also pass target_timeline_frame; those should be left alone if they don't call the duplicate_as_new_generation RPC.
- T5 (upload pipeline return types) touches shared infrastructure. Verify early that createGenerationForUploadedImage actually returns the full row (check the .select() call). If it doesn't, you'll need to add .select('*').single() before the rest of the work makes sense.

**Key judgment calls:**
- For useStableValue in T3: search the codebase first for existing shallow-compare hooks (useDeepCompareMemo, useShallowCompare, etc.). If one exists, use it. If not, write it inline in TimelineEditor.tsx — do NOT create a new shared utility file per CLAUDE.md guidance.
- For T2 optimistic UI: the plan says SQL authority > optimistic preview. If the duplicate action currently shows instant visual feedback of the new frame position, accept a brief flash/repositioning when the RPC returns. Don't add complexity to maintain pixel-perfect optimistic placement.
- For T5 mapJoinedShotGenerationToRow cleanup: grep for all callers before removing. If it's used by other paths (e.g., createShotWithPromptPath), keep it.

plan_steps_covered.plan_step_summary: Step 1: Apply stopPropagation workaround to TimelineCanvas shot group menu
Step 2: Stop passing pre-computed target_timeline_frame from travel-between-images duplicate path, make SQL authoritative
Step 3: Extract stable asset→generationId mapping from registry to fix EffectRegistry re-render issue
Step 4: Remove shotActionsSnapshotRef workaround from ClipAction.tsx
Step 5: Have upload pipeline return full generation metadata, eliminate post-upload fetch in createShotWithFilesPath

validation.completeness_notes: T6 is the mandatory final test/verification task, not mapped to a specific plan step but required by the finalize template. All 5 plan steps are covered by T1-T5. The plan's validation order (run tests after each phase) is collapsed into a single final test task T6 since all phases are independent and tests can be run together at the end.
